skill.name: video-inpainting
skill.description: Region edits across video frames on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — remove an object that appears across many frames, clean up wires or watermarks, replace a region with matching motion. Routes across Wan 2-7 edit-video (default, prompt-driven region edits with spatial language), Lucy Edit Restyle (identity-stable region-aware restyle), and Seedream 4-0 edit-sequential (when treating the clip as a frame stack).
skill.summary: Video inpainting router for the RunComfy CLI — removes objects, cleans watermarks, and patches regions across video frames using prompt-driven AI models.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Legitimate video inpainting skill with narrow bash scope (Bash(runcomfy *)). The 38 'external_commands' and 23 'network' static findings are false positives — backticks are markdown formatting and URLs are documentation links to runcomfy.com. The 'Windows SAM database' detection is a false positive matching 'SAM2' (Segment Anything Model). Token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600 is explicitly documented.

low_findings.title: Token storage in home directory
low_findings.description: The skill stores API tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json (line 160). This is explicitly documented with mode 0600 permissions, and is standard practice for CLI tools. The path is in the user's config directory, not a system-sensitive location.

low_findings.title: Network access to external API
low_findings.description: The skill makes outbound requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and related domains (documented on line 165). This is expected behavior for a cloud-based video processing service. The skill explicitly states no telemetry is collected.

content.user_title: Remove Objects from Video Clips with AI
content.value_statement: Removing objects, watermarks, or unwanted elements from video clips is difficult with standard editing tools. This skill routes your request to the right AI model on RunComfy for prompt-driven video inpainting across frames.
actual_capabilities: Routes video inpainting requests to Wan 2-7 Edit-Video, Lucy Edit Restyle, or Seedream 4-0 Edit-Sequential
Removes objects appearing across multiple video frames using prompt-driven spatial language
Cleans up watermarks, logos, wires, and passing background people from video clips
Replaces regions with matching motion that preserves the rest of the video content
Supports identity-locked restyles for outfit and object swaps that track across frames
Outputs processed video files to a specified output directory via the runcomfy CLI
limitations: Prompt-driven region targeting is not pixel-precise — mask propagation requires ComfyUI workflows
Long clips may show temporal consistency degradation with Seedream 4-0 sequential mode
Compound edits (remove A and replace B) in a single call may drift; sequential passes recommended
Image inpainting (single stills) and video outpainting are handled by separate skills

use_cases.title: Watermark and logo removal
use_cases.description: Remove watermarks, logos, or branding overlays that appear throughout a video clip using spatial language prompts.

use_cases.title: Background object cleanup
use_cases.description: Clean up passing people, wires, or distracting elements from the background of a video clip while preserving the main subject.

use_cases.title: Region replacement with motion
use_cases.description: Replace a specific region across frames (sky, object, surface) with new content that matches the existing motion of the clip.

prompt_templates.title: Remove a watermark
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner across all frames. Preserve all other content exactly. Match the background where the watermark was.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up stock footage or removing branded overlays

prompt_templates.title: Remove a passing person
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the person walking in the background, fill with matching environment. Preserve the main subject and all other content exactly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up location footage with incidental passersby

prompt_templates.title: Replace a region with new content
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the sky in the upper half of the frame with a sunset. Preserve the subject and all foreground content exactly. Match lighting and color tone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Altering environmental elements while keeping the subject intact

prompt_templates.title: Identity-locked outfit swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the subject's jacket to a red leather jacket. Preserve identity, face, and all other content exactly. Maintain consistent appearance across all frames.
prompt_templates.scenario: Lightweight outfit or object replacement that needs to track across frames

output_examples.input: Video clip with a watermark in the bottom-right corner, prompt: 'Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner across all frames. Preserve all other content exactly.'
output: Watermark cleanly removed from all frames
Background filled to match surrounding content
No changes to the main video content or composition

output_examples.input: Footage with a person walking through the background, prompt: 'Remove the person walking in the background, fill with matching environment.'
output: Processed video with the background person removed
Environment reconstructed to match the original setting
Temporal consistency maintained across all frames

best_practices: Use spatial language to describe the target region (e.g., 'bottom-right corner', 'the cables overhead') rather than pixel coordinates
Lead prompts with 'Preserve all other content exactly' to prevent unintended restyling of the full frame
Split compound edits into sequential passes — one change per call produces more reliable results
anti_patterns: Do not attempt pixel-precise mask propagation via this skill — use ComfyUI workflows for surgical masking
Do not combine multiple unrelated edits in a single prompt call — results tend to drift from intent
Do not use Seedream 4-0 sequential mode for long clips — temporal consistency degrades on motion-heavy footage

faq.question: What AI models does this skill route to?
faq.answer: The skill routes to Wan 2-7 Edit-Video (default for prompt-driven region edits), Lucy Edit Restyle (identity-locked restyles), and Seedream 4-0 Edit-Sequential (frame-stack treatment).

faq.question: Can this skill do pixel-precise mask-based inpainting?
faq.answer: No. The CLI endpoints resolve the target region from spatial language in the prompt. For pixel-precise mask propagation, use dedicated ComfyUI workflows such as LTX 2-3 inpaint or SAM2 segmentation tracking.

faq.question: How are API tokens stored?
faq.answer: The runcomfy login command stores the API token at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file mode 0600. In CI or containers, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What is the bash scope for this skill?
faq.answer: The skill is restricted to Bash(runcomfy *) only. No other shell commands are permitted through the skill's allowed-tools configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill send telemetry or collect data?
faq.answer: No. The skill only communicates with model-api.runcomfy.net and related runcomfy domains. No telemetry is collected or sent to third parties.

faq.question: Can I remove multiple objects in one call?
faq.answer: It is not recommended. Compound edits (remove A and replace B) tend to drift from the intended result. Split the work into sequential edit passes for reliable output.
